title: Welcome, Faculty, and the Culture of Speaking Up

objectives.outcome: Section Head and core NCC faculty and the scope of attending coverage
NCC as a multidisciplinary regionalized team-based service
Barrow NCC Resources website and key orientation materials

didactic_sections.heading: Why This Lesson Matters
key_points: Speak-up culture is institutional, not optional

didactic_sections.heading: Who You Are Joining
key_points: Section Head: Ruchira Jha, MD MSc
Core faculty: Fiona Lynch, Patrick Crooks, Nassim Matin, Aditya Kumar
Broader committee includes Mangalampalli, Nelson, Haller, Phuah, Sisniega
BNI Handbook 2026-2027 by Mangalampalli/Kumar/Nelson is the pocket reference
APPs are partners — not coverage — for residents and attendings

didactic_sections.heading: Our Culture: Speak Up
key_points: Something went or is about to go wrong → escalate
Culture violations reportable to APP lead, Section Head, HR

didactic_sections.heading: Multidisciplinary by Design
key_points: Consults cohorted if NCC problems dominate
Outside-hospital transfers never delayed for bed criteria

linked_protocols: Admission Diagnosis Guidelines for NCC Pod 4NNC
nursing_considerations: RNs on 4NBC are subspecialty-trained for NCC; rely on their pattern recognition for subtle exam changes
Bedside RN is the first to call you for changes; treat their calls as data, not noise
pharm_considerations: Dedicated NCC pharmacy presence enables protocolized drips (hypertonic saline, nicardipine, anesthetic infusions); identify the pharmacist on your first shift

visuals.subject: Faculty headshots with names and titles
BNI/SJHMC organizational chart for NCC
visuals.notes: Shows Ice/Fire teams, attending coverage, APP, residents, fellows, pharmacy, RT, RN, charge

assessment.item: Explain why patients are cohorted on 4NBC
